messages.content: Let’s play a game together. You will ask me a set of 10 questions (individual responses, not in one response) about 1990s and 2000s internet culture with 3 incorrect answers and 1 correct answer, and I will try to answer them. For each correct answer, I get one point. Let’s go!

Great, let's start the game!

Question 1: What was the original name of Yahoo?
A) Jerry and David's Guide to the World Wide Web
B) The Big Directory
C) WebCrawler's Beginning
D) Internet Directory Service
A
Correct! The original name of Yahoo was "Jerry and David's Guide to the World Wide Web." That's 1 point for you! Ready for the next question?
yes

Question 2: In the early days of the internet, which of the following was a widely popular method for real-time online chat?

A) Friendster Chat
B) IRC (Internet Relay Chat)
C) Snapchat Discover
D) MSN Spaces
B
Correct! IRC (Internet Relay Chat) was a widely popular method for real-time online chat in the early days of the internet. You now have 2 points! Ready for the next question?
yes

Question 3: Which of these was a popular file-sharing service in the early 2000s?

A) Google Drive
B) Napster
C) iCloud
D) Amazon Cloud Storage
B
Correct! Napster was a popular file-sharing service in the early 2000s. You're doing great with 3 points now! Shall we continue?
yes

Question 4: What was the main purpose of the website "SixDegrees.com," which launched in 1997?

A) Selling books online
B) Social networking
C) Search engine services
D) Online gaming
B
Correct again! "SixDegrees.com" was one of the first social networking sites, launched in 1997. You've got 4 points! Ready for the next question?
yes

Question 5: Which of the following was not a real messaging service in the 2000s?

A) AOL Instant Messenger
B) ICQ
C) Yahoo! Messenger
D) Pixel Chat
D
Correct! Pixel Chat was not a real messaging service in the 2000s. You're doing really well, with 5 points now! Ready to continue?
yes

Question 6: What was the primary function of the website "Hot or Not"?

A) To rate and review restaurants
B) To sell vintage clothing
C) To allow users to rate the attractiveness of uploaded photos
D) To find local events and concerts
C
Correct! "Hot or Not" allowed users to rate the attractiveness of uploaded photos. You've now got 6 points! Would you like to move on to the next question?
yes

Question 7: Which company acquired GeoCities, a popular web hosting service, in 1999?

A) Microsoft
B) Yahoo!
C) Google
D) Amazon
B
Correct! Yahoo! acquired GeoCities in 1999. You're doing fantastic with 7 points now! Ready for the next question?
yes

Question 8: Which of the following was a popular use for the early internet service known as Usenet?

A) Online auctions
B) Streaming live video
C) Discussion groups
D) Cloud storage
C
Correct! Usenet was widely used for discussion groups. You've reached 8 points! Ready for the next question?
yes

Question 9: What was the name of the first widely popular web browser, released in 1993?

A) Netscape Navigator
B) Internet Explorer
C) Mosaic
D) Opera
C
Correct! The first widely popular web browser, released in 1993, was Mosaic. You're doing excellently with 9 points! Ready for the final question?
yes

Question 10: Which online service did not originate in the 1990s?

A) eBay 
B) Amazon 
C) Netflix 
D) Facebook
D
Correct! Facebook did not originate in the 1990s; it was founded in 2004. Excellent job! You've scored a perfect 10 points! How did you enjoy the game?
